Avoid dust and allergens.
Antihistamines may help if allergies are the cause.
Ear pain can be caused by infection or wax buildup.
Warm compress may help relieve pain.
Consult a doctor if pain is severe.
Breathing problems can be serious.
If severe seek emergency medical help immediately.
Please contact a healthcare professional.

Avoid smoking and reduce stress.

I'm SJ AI, a medical assistant chatbot that provides health information.
I'm an AI assistant trained to help with medical questions.
I can provide information about symptoms and common illnesses, but I do not replace a real doctor.
Fever usually indicates the body is fighting an infection.
Stay hydrated and rest. Monitor your temperature.
If your fever is very high, accompanied by a stiff neck, or lasts more than 3 days, consult a doctor immediately.
Headaches can be caused by stress, dehydration, lack of sleep, or illness.
Try resting in a dark, quiet room and drinking water.
If headaches are sudden and severe, or frequent, please consult a doctor.
A cough may be caused by an infection, allergies, or throat irritation.
Drink warm fluids, use a humidifier, and rest your throat.
If your cough lasts more than 2 weeks or produces blood, seek medical advice.
A sore throat is commonly caused by viral infections like the common cold.
Warm salt water gargles and throat lozenges can help relieve pain.
Drink plenty of warm fluids and get some rest.
A runny or stuffy nose is usually caused by a cold, allergies, or sinus issues.
Steam inhalation and staying hydrated may help relieve symptoms.
Rest and give your body time to fight off any potential cold virus.

patterns: Why do I sneeze a lot
responses: Sneezing is often caused by allergies, dust, or respiratory infections.
Try to identify and avoid allergens like pollen, pet dander, or dust.
Over-the-counter antihistamines may help if allergies are the cause.
Ear pain can be caused by infections, fluid buildup, or wax impaction.
A warm compress against the ear may help relieve mild pain.
Consult a doctor if the pain is severe, lasts more than a day, or is accompanied by a fever.
Breathing problems can be a sign of a serious medical emergency.
If you are experiencing severe shortness of breath, seek emergency medical help immediately.
Please contact a healthcare professional or go to the nearest emergency room.
Stomach pain can be caused by indigestion, food poisoning, a stomach bug, or stress.
Stay hydrated by taking small sips of water or clear broths. Avoid solid, spicy, or greasy food for a few hours.
If the pain is severe, you are unable to keep fluids down for 24 hours, or you see blood, seek immediate medical attention.
Fatigue can result from lack of sleep, poor diet, stress, or underlying conditions like anemia or thyroid issues.
Ensure you are getting 7-9 hours of sleep, eating a balanced diet, and staying hydrated.
If severe fatigue persists for weeks despite lifestyle changes, consider consulting a doctor.
Rashes can be triggered by allergies, infections, heat, or contact with irritants.
Avoid scratching to prevent infection. A cool compress or over-the-counter hydrocortisone cream may help soothe the itch.
If the rash spreads rapidly, is very painful, or comes with a fever, see a healthcare provider.
Muscle and joint pain are often caused by tension, overuse, minor injuries, or viral infections like the flu.
Rest the affected area, apply ice for acute injuries, and consider gentle stretching.
If the pain is severe, accompanied by swelling, or doesn't improve after a few days, consult a physician.
It's very important to take mental health seriously. You are not alone.
Practice deep breathing, meditation, or light exercise to help manage immediate stress and anxiety.
Consider talking to a therapist or counselor. If you are in crisis, please reach out to a local mental health helpline immediately.

patterns: Waking up in the middle of the night
responses: Try to maintain a consistent sleep schedule and limit screen time for at least an hour before bed.
Avoid caffeine, heavy meals, and vigorous exercise late in the evening.
Creating a dark, quiet, and cool sleep environment can help. If problems persist, consult a doctor.
For minor burns, immediately run cool (not ice cold) water over the area for 10-15 minutes.
Do not apply ice, butter, or oil to the burn. Cover it loosely with a sterile, non-stick bandage.
If the burn is large, on your face, or starts blistering heavily, seek medical attention.
Common COVID-19 symptoms include fever, dry cough, fatigue, and loss of taste or smell.
Consider taking a rapid test and isolating if symptoms appear.
Contact healthcare services if symptoms worsen, especially if you have trouble breathing.
Eat a balanced diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and whole grains. Exercise regularly.
Get 7-9 hours of sleep per night and drink plenty of water throughout the day.
Avoid smoking, limit alcohol intake, and find healthy ways to manage stress.
Over-the-counter medicines like paracetamol or ibuprofen may help mild symptoms like pain or fever.
Always read the label and follow dosage instructions carefully.
I am an AI, so I cannot prescribe medication. Consult a pharmacist or doctor before taking new medication.
WARNING: This sounds like a medical emergency.
Please call your local emergency services (like 911 or your country's equivalent) immediately.
Do not wait. Seek urgent medical help right away.
I can help you understand your symptoms better. To start, what is your primary symptom?
I'm sorry to hear you're not feeling well. What is the main issue bothering you today?
Thank you. Experiencing this for that long helps narrow it down. Are you experiencing any other symptoms alongside it?

patterns: My chest is in severe pain
I think I'm having a heart attack
My arm is numb and I can't speak
responses: 🚨 WARNING: Your symptoms indicate a potential medical emergency. Please call 911 or your local emergency number immediately. Do not wait.

patterns: Are my blood test results back?

responses: I can check your patient portal for recent lab results. Please provide your secure Patient ID or Date of Birth to verify your identity.
